neutral.comment: Oh? How do I go about activating the 3 hour trial? I didn't get any prompt regarding it
neutral.parent: By the way, there are some sort of unavoidable issues with ios force quitting background apps if there is enough memory pressure on the phone (using things like the camera etc.) I've put in some safeguards but it won't prevent all cases. I'm still working out a way to make it even better (and it will try to restart once you've moved a certain distance since ios allows that feature), but yeah, it could be a deal breaker for some people. There are other apps vaguely similar to this and I've tested them and they all have this same issue. The only foolproof thing I could think of is some sort of push notification when then app is offloaded, but that could be annoying in its own way As for the pro features there is a 3 hour trial at the beginning. Right now the pro features are just no ads and statistics. Planning on building those out though also. Leaderboards will also be pro

positive.comment: Thank you for your thorough response! I agree there is the cost of travel aspect to consider. I’ll have to carefully think about how to handle that. positive.comment: Hey thanks for the feedback! And I don't think I collect email, unless there's a way through App Store Connect to get user email info but I'd think that would not exist for privacy purposes
positive.parent: I downloaded and fired it up. From my initial 30 second experience: - I like the design a lot, very cool. It feels fresh and not derivative. If you are vibe coding the UI, great job. - When I see my initial location, I would probably want to engage more if there were some sort of score or sort of resource or location I could claim. Maybe think a bit about Pokemon Go mechanics to make this more exciting to users. However, that would put you in the business of a multiuser backend - The UI transition from macro to micro gets a little jarring when the bigger region fades out entirely and you are just looking at the micro. Maybe work on that a little bit for ideal UX You can email me directly from your user info database if you want to chat more.

positive.comment: Used Sora with a bunch of iteration to make the logo. I kind of backed into it b/c I found this which I use in one of the menus: and I thought it felt out of place unless I used it to inspire the logo lol

positive.comment: No, but I would definitely consider implementing that if there were interest, seems like a good idea

positive.parent: Okay. Love it. Life time license bought. I could just not be able to find it but please add the ability to turn off all sound under settings. positive.comment: Maybe you could link it to social media and unlock places by having friends there, could be cool
positive.parent: Yeah, I basically have 2 "levels" of tile resolution... the big ones I sized so that someone who dedicated a lot of effort to visiting everywhere could feasibly complete it. The smaller level is more for like "I visited all of the tiles in my home major tile", or showing where you've been like you say. But anyway, that's good feedback, thanks! Maybe I could have challenges/milestones like visiting all the major tiles in the US for example, and then a set of milestones that actually is achievable for the completionists

negative.comment: I'm considering this. I was torn between "overcomplicating" it vs making the tile size not what most people like. For what it's worth I selected the small tiles based on my idea of where someone would be able to go assuming they have access to a car. Almost like for road trippers for example. That's not everyone in all parts of the world though, but it is something doable at most levels of wealth at least in the US, though I could be wrong. I'm hesitant to make it them too small b/c then it's going to be impossible to ever unlock all of the tiles in a section (you can't for example walk into every building or house, that would be illegal). But if it's a popular enough request I'll probably do it
negative.parent: Any plans to make it another layer deeper? I live in a very dense city and was hoping there is a deeper layer since there are many things to explore around where I'm at.

positive.comment: By the way, there are some sort of unavoidable issues with ios force quitting background apps if there is enough memory pressure on the phone (using things like the camera etc.) I've put in some safeguards but it won't prevent all cases. I'm still working out a way to make it even better (and it will try to restart once you've moved a certain distance since ios allows that feature), but yeah, it could be a deal breaker for some people. There are other apps vaguely similar to this and I've tested them and they all have this same issue. The only foolproof thing I could think of is some sort of push notification when then app is offloaded, but that could be annoying in its own way As for the pro features there is a 3 hour trial at the beginning. Right now the pro features are just no ads and statistics. Planning on building those out though also. Leaderboards will also be pro
